human_verification.notes: Reviewed the architecture-and-engineering-services design-quality package for specialty-boundary quality, authority-source quality, milestone-package realism, BIM and review-comment evidence discipline, licensed-authority separation, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to architecture-and-engineering design-quality evidence review, milestone hold or release discipline, review-comment and BIM evidence handling, and corrective-action escalation rather than final licensed approval.

Authority sources cover GSA design and commissioning guidance, USACE A-E, DrChecks, and quality-risk workflow materials, NIBS BIM control guidance, WBDG constructability review, and FAR architect-engineer boundary policy with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat coordination defects, control bypass attempts, unresolved holds, licensed-authority escalation, overgeneralized design requests, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, adjacent-lane routing, and unsupported closure attempts.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/architecture-and-engineering-services/design-quality-specialist/ to preserve the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, code and permitting, contractual, and safety-critical authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated architecture-and-engineering design-quality overlay where final licensed design approval, code authority, permit authority, and contractual signoff remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
